title: Temporal Source Grounding
question: When does the vocal first start to play?
observation: MusT exactly localizes the vocal onset at 2:00, while the baseline models predict substantially earlier timestamps.

title: Transition-Aware Descrption
question: Describe the musical change at 1:22 in detail.
ground_truth_text: As the harmony moves away from its previous tonal color toward a G-major-related sound, the drums ease back, making the rhythmic presence more subdued.

predictions.text: The harmony shifts to G major as the drums ease back, softening their attack while the bass disappears from the mix.
predictions.observation: Captures the G-major-related harmonic movement and the drums easing back, matching the subdued rhythmic transition.

predictions.text: Drums and bass enter, creating a more dynamic rhythmic texture.
predictions.observation: Predicts drums and bass entering, while the reference says the drums ease back and the rhythm becomes more subdued.

predictions.text: The music moves into a more intense section with a driving rhythm.
predictions.observation: Describes an intensification, but the ground truth describes reduced rhythmic presence.

observation: MusT captures both the harmonic shift toward G major and the reduced drum/rhythmic intensity, while the selected baselines predict the opposite direction of change.
